summary: Comprehensive e2e testing of TaskFlow MVP. 28/28 backend pytest tests pass. Frontend flows validated: admin/staff login, task CRUD, subtasks, comments, activity log, dashboard chart, staff management, RBAC route guard, theme toggle. Two minor issues observed (label UI create + one CORS config concern).

minor.issue: allow_credentials=True combined with allow_origins=['*'] is invalid per spec; browsers block cookies. Works currently in preview because origin is same host, but should list explicit origins for production.
secure=False hardcoded — should be True on HTTPS in production.
Line 469 `'global': user.get('role') == 'admin' and False` always evaluates to False; dead expression, likely a bug intent to allow admin to create global labels.
No rate limiting / account lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook expected lockout after 5 fails).

ui_bugs.issue: Login welcome toast overlaps the 'Create Task' FAB in top-right for ~4s, transiently blocking clicks. Not a blocker; retry works after toast dismisses.

integration_issues.issue: Console shows 'WebSocket closed before connection established' warnings on every page. Notifications GET fallback works (verified via API), so live push may be flaky/broken behind the ingress. Recommend a short polling fallback (or ping/pong keep-alive).

issues: Priority Distribution donut chart renders but P4 slice is not visible when all tasks are P1 — acceptable.
FAB (Create Task) sits at same z-region as Sonner toaster; may be transiently covered.

action_items: Fix CORS: set explicit allowed origins instead of '*' when allow_credentials=True.
Set cookies secure=True (or driven by env) since app is served over HTTPS.
Fix label 'global' assignment logic in POST /api/labels (server.py:469).
Consider adding rate limiting / lockout on /api/auth/login.
Investigate WebSocket close-before-open; consider polling fallback for notifications.
Reposition Sonner toaster so it does not overlap the top-right FAB (e.g., bottom-right, or add margin so FAB stays clickable).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1086 lines — split into routers (auth, users, tasks, labels, comments, attachments, activity, notifications, dashboard) for maintainability.
create_label: `'global': user.get('role') == 'admin' and False` is redundant; either remove or implement the admin-global-labels feature.
CORS allow_credentials with '*' origins violates spec — will break real browsers on cross-origin.
download_attachment allows ?auth= query token — acceptable for image previews but logs may leak tokens; note as an intentional trade-off.
Dashboard endpoint runs N+4 count_documents per user (line 954-968); acceptable at MVP scale, but consider aggregation pipeline.
Activity is correctly immutable (no PATCH/DELETE route).

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all critical flows pass; only WS live push flaky and label-create UI selector unresolved)

seed_data_creation: Admin user auto-seeded on backend startup. Test cleanup fixture removes any TEST_* users, tasks, labels created during pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py cover all endpoints (auth, users, tasks incl. RBAC, subtasks, labels, comments, activity, notifications, dashboard) — 28 passing. If main agent modifies API contracts, re-run: `python -m pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. For frontend, the FAB at [data-testid=floating-create-btn] is the 'Create Task' button top-right — wait ~4s after login for Sonner toast to dismiss before clicking, OR use force=True with explicit position. Labels page create-button flow needs explicit data-testids (add-label-btn, label-name-input, label-save-btn) — currently generic buttons make it hard to test.

rca_of_notable_findings.modal_open_timing: First attempt failing waiting for [data-testid=create-task-modal] was caused by Sonner 'Welcome back' toast being z-index above the FAB for ~4 seconds after login. Adding await page.wait_for_timeout(4500) between login and FAB click resolves it. Consider re-positioning toaster or lowering its pointer-events.
rca_of_notable_findings.cors_credentials_wildcard: server.py lines 1079-1085 set allow_credentials=True with allow_origins=['*'] (from CORS_ORIGINS='*'). Fetch spec forbids '*' with credentials; today it works because backend is same-origin behind ingress, but a real cross-origin frontend build would break.
